summary: Well-structured test suite with good TDD progression covering all three retention policies, dry-run mode, combined scenarios, and input edge cases. Bats tests use clear fixture patterns with setup/teardown, while act integration tests provide format-exact assertions. Main gaps: no testing of invalid argument values (--max-age-days='abc'), limited boundary condition coverage (e.g., size=0, keep-latest=0), and bats assertions rely on substring matching rather than exact format validation. The act harness rigorously validates tab-separated output format in 3 integration cases. Would catch policy logic regressions and most input parsing failures, but might miss error handling regressions and output format changes.
